# reducer-handler-budgets.txt — B-9 (#3802): per-materialization-handler latency
# budgets for the credential-free reducer micro-benchmarks.
#
# Each row is the absolute ns/op ceiling for one reducer materialization or
# correlation handler benchmark. scripts/verify-reducer-perf-gate.sh runs each
# benchmark credential-free (no Postgres/NornicDB), takes the median ns/op across
# samples, and fails (or warns) when the median exceeds the budget.
#
# This is an ABSOLUTE-ceiling gate, complementary to the benchstat-relative
# bench-regression gate (B-2 / #3795). It answers the #3802 acceptance directly:
# "CI asserts main meets per-handler budgets." Splitting files (Epic D) does not
# make materialization faster, so these handler budgets hold regardless of the
# repo/monorepo layout.
#
# Budget = round(median_baseline * 1.50). The 1.50x headroom (wider than the
# benchstat gate's 1.10x) is deliberate: this is a single-run absolute ceiling
# evaluated on a shared CI runner of a DIFFERENT class than where the baseline
# was captured (darwin/arm64 Apple M4 Pro, count=6, benchtime=100ms), so it must
# absorb both cross-class CPU difference and per-run noise without flaking. The
# baseline column records the measured median that the ceiling was derived from.
#
# Baseline source: locally measured on darwin/arm64 (Apple M4 Pro), go test
# ./internal/reducer -benchtime=100ms -count=6, 2026-06-28, branch
# claude/b9-reducer-gate-3802. Because that is NOT the CI ubuntu-latest runner
# class, the CI gate runs ADVISORY (REDUCER_PERF_ENFORCE=false) until a CI-class
# baseline is recaptured — the same shared-runner-variance reasoning the B-2
# bench-regression gate documents. Refresh via
# scripts/refresh-reducer-handler-budgets.sh on the enforcement runner, review
# the diff, then flip the workflow to REDUCER_PERF_ENFORCE=true.
#
# #3710/#3725 baseline win locked separately in
# docs/public/reference/reducer-claim-latency-gate.md (the deferred relationship
# fact-LOAD long pole, measured 4.3x/3.9x handler + ~2.4x full-corpus wall-clock).
#
# Columns: benchmark<TAB>budget_ns<TAB>baseline_ns
# Lines beginning with '#' and blank lines are ignored.
BenchmarkExtractCloudResourceNodeRows	16100000	10741623
BenchmarkExtractGCPCloudResourceNodeRows	15800000	10550352
BenchmarkExtractKubernetesWorkloadNodeRows	8100000	5413844
BenchmarkExtractAWSRelationshipEdgeRows	37000000	24690270
BenchmarkExtractGCPRelationshipEdgeRows	41500000	27680020
BenchmarkExtractKubernetesCorrelationEdgeRows	13800000	9174514
BenchmarkSecretsIAMGCPGrantObservations	8300000	5546848
BenchmarkBuildServiceCatalogCorrelationDecisionsHighCardinalityFanout	9200000	6120469
BenchmarkValueFlowFixpointFull	30600000	20427691
BenchmarkValueFlowFixpointIncrementalCached	18000000	11982886
# #5457 graph-provenance-edges row builders: baseline captured on darwin/arm64
# (Apple M5 Max, a different CPU class than the file's original M4 Pro
# baseline runs), go test ./internal/reducer -bench=... -benchtime=100ms
# -count=6, 2026-07-23, median of 6 samples.
BenchmarkPackageOwnershipPublishesRows	703526	469017
BenchmarkPackagePublicationPublishesRows	693965	462643
BenchmarkContainerImageBuiltFromRows	875376	583584
